text.title: Code Review Antipatterns
text.summary: LGTM culture, bikeshedding, reviewer fatigue, agent false positives, and review-after-merge.

antipattern.description: LGTM culture: reviewers approve without commenting, evidenced by zero review comments across many merged PRs.
antipattern.reason: Zero-comment approvals indicate rubber-stamping. Counter: required review templates, checklists, or a team norm that silent approval is not valid for PRs above a certain size.

antipattern.description: Style debates in review comments for formatting decisions that a linter could decide automatically.
antipattern.reason: Style reviews waste reviewer and author time on zero-signal debates. Delegate all formatting to Prettier/ruff/gofmt; enforce in CI; review only logic.

antipattern.description: PRs sitting open for >3 days, becoming stale with conflicts and requiring reviewers to start over.
antipattern.reason: Long-lived PRs accumulate merge conflicts and become unreviewable. Time-bound: stale-after-72h warning, auto-close-after-14d. Split large work into smaller sequential PRs.

antipattern.description: Agent reviewer auto-approving PRs when CI passes.
antipattern.reason: Green CI means the code is syntactically correct and tests pass — not that the design is sound. Configure agent reviewers to comment-only; require at least one human approval before merge.

antipattern.description: Agent reviewer posting 100+ comments on a single PR, causing engineers to ignore all agent feedback.
antipattern.reason: Comment flooding trains engineers to dismiss agent reviews entirely. Cap output at 30 findings by severity; surface only blockers and majors in the summary.

antipattern.description: Review-after-merge: GitHub allows merging without required reviews; team adopts this practice "temporarily".
antipattern.reason: Defects merged this way ship. Enforce branch protection: `required_pull_request_reviews=1`, `dismiss_stale_reviews_on_push=true`, `require_code_owner_reviews=true`.

antipattern.description: Agent reviewer operates from a stale checkout, reviewing HEAD~3 instead of the current branch tip.
antipattern.reason: Reviewing old commits produces false findings about "missing" changes that are already present in newer commits. Always re-fetch latest before generating comments.
